title: DynMoLE: Boosting Mixture of LoRA Experts Fine-Tuning with a Hybrid Routing Mechanism

introduction.topic: Instruction-based fine-tuning of large language models for downstream tasks
introduction.focus: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods, particularly LoRA and Mixture of LoRA Experts (MoLE)
introduction.problem: Current routing mechanisms in MoLE are mostly derived from standard MoE models and don't account for varying expert requirements across transformer layers

methodology.goal: Address challenges in MoLE routing to improve model performance

challenges.name: Inconsistent Expert Selection
challenges.description: Flat probability distributions lead to similar inputs activating different experts, resulting in unstable expert training

challenges.name: Varied Expert Requirements
challenges.description: Different transformer layers have different expert requirements, leading to uneven expert loads

routing_mechanisms.description: Activates all expert networks for each input token
Activates only a subset of experts
routing_mechanisms.disadvantage: Often results in imbalanced workload among experts

results.performance: Outperforms state-of-the-art MoLE method, MoLA, by 2.3%
results.evaluation: Comprehensive ablation study to evaluate contributions of key components

motivation: Design a hybrid routing approach that considers diverse and complex factors affecting model performance to provide a more complete solution for MoE and MoLE models
